understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by maneuvering the pieces so that a pushable block/object ends up on the goal tile(s) marked with a '+' (or otherwise satisfy the engine’s WIN condition), progressing through all levels within the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action shifts the currently selected piece exactly one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction using pixel coordinates like [x,y]. If a pushable block is adjacent in the movement direction and the space beyond is free, the selected piece can push it along; solid walls/obstacles prevent movement/pushing when they block the destination. Some boundaries can be ambiguous, so attempted moves are sometimes needed to confirm behavior. When multiple controllable pieces exist, CLICK switches control to the selectable located at the given pixel position, making that piece the one that moves on subsequent MOVE actions.
